segments: The Plaza Macao and Four Seasons Macao

dependencies: Dependence on third-party hotel operators and management companies for hotel operations
Reliance on third-party vendors for gaming operating systems and technology services
Dependence on construction contractors and subcontractors for development projects
risks: Construction contractors may be unable to meet obligations leading to increased costs
Shortages of materials or skilled labor could cause cost overruns and delays
Disruptions in technology services from third parties could impact operations
Foreign workers seconded to contractors under government labor quotas create employer obligations

regulatory_risks: Gaming concession in Macao expires December 31, 2032 and may not be renewed
Macao government can unilaterally terminate gaming concession for non-compliance
Singapore casino license expires April 2025 and renewal not guaranteed
Singapore government can terminate casino concession on public interest grounds
Macao government requires approval for transfers of shares, debt transactions, and financial decisions
Compliance with Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and anti-corruption laws
Anti-money laundering laws and regulations in Singapore and Macao
Gaming debts may not be enforceable in certain jurisdictions
Macao government can require modifications to property plans and specifications
Singapore government can impose financial penalties up to 10% of annual gross gaming revenue for serious breaches
Personal Information Protection Law of the PRC (PIPL) may apply extraterritorially to Macao operations
Cybersecurity Review Measures in China may require review by CAC
New York State casino license required for Nassau Coliseum development with no assurance of approval
Environmental regulations regarding carbon emissions, plastic use, and waste disposal
Tax law changes including OECD BEPS project and minimum tax rates
Data security and privacy regulations including PIPL and cybersecurity requirements
Labor and immigration restrictions in Macao affecting ability to hire non-resident workers
Macao government can revise land concession rent amounts every five years
Investment Plan requirements under Macao Concession totaling $4.48 billion by 2032
Singapore MBS Expansion Project completion deadline of July 8, 2029 subject to government approval for extensions

sanction_risks: Chinese government policies on travel and currency movements could disrupt visitor numbers from mainland China
Restrictions on exit visas for mainland China residents could reduce Macao visitation
Foreign exchange and capital control restrictions could be imposed on Macao and Hong Kong operations
Restrictions on remittance of Renminbi from mainland China could impede gaming patron flow
Chinese government could extend oversight and control applicable in mainland China to Macao and Hong Kong operations
CAC could restrict or prohibit provision of personal information to the company
Potential application of mainland China laws and regulations to Macao operations under 'one country, two systems'
Economic, political and legal developments in Macao subject to Chinese government influence
